course.title: Build and Ship a Production Feed Aggregator in TypeScript 7 and Node 24
course.description: Advanced TypeScript backend learners ready to integrate syndication standards, hostile XML, runtime validation, Fetch and Promise ownership, PostgreSQL, Express, accessible presentation, security, testing, deployment, and recovery into one cumulative service. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a cumulative multi-tenant rss 2.0 and atom 1.0 service from authorized discovery through secure runtime validation, publisher-friendly ingestion, postgresql search, express apis, and an accessible content-safe reader through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate typescript 7 and node 24 source-to-emission evidence, fast-xml-parser hardening, zod admission, fetch streams and abortsignal ownership, pg transactions, durable scheduling, fenced workers, asynclocalstorage telemetry, security, testing, packaging, deployment, backup, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate original deterministic browser models plus controlled compiler, emitted node, loopback http, postgresql, handle, subprocess, container, load, accessibility, backup, restore, and release transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five distinct stakeholder projects and a cumulative production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

credential.title: Build and Ship a Production Feed Aggregator in TypeScript 7 and Node 24 Certificate

modules.title: Reader Outcomes, Publisher Respect, and Product Contract
modules.description: A team proposes a blog aggregator from a feature list but has not defined reader decisions, publisher obligations, freshness, content harm, accessibility, privacy, or mastery evidence. Learners cumulatively produce inclusive feed-reader product contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define reader population, decision, task journey, accepted delay, failure harm, and observable learning or research value.
Specify respectful identification, fetch cadence, caching, correction, deletion, source pause, and incident contact behavior.
Separate source discovery, ingestion, storage, scheduling, API, reader state, and presentation with explicit accepted unknowns.

modules.title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, ESM, Dependencies, and Clean Reproduction
modules.description: The prototype runs through a development loader with floating packages, unchecked emitted JavaScript, no migration version, warm installs, and uncertain Node compatibility. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible TypeScript feed-service repository through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Pin Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 plus the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility surface, ESM semantics, package manager, and platform matrix.
Pin Express 5.2.1, pg 8.22.0, fast-xml-parser 5.10.0, Zod 4.4.3, Pino 10.3.1, PostgreSQL 18.4, and migration tooling from reviewed sources.
Bind strict source, configuration, generated migrations or schemas, source maps, emitted JavaScript, package exports, and startup entry.

modules.title: Ports, Immutable Domain Values, Promise Ownership, and Lifecycle
modules.description: Modules import global pools and fetch directly, promises float, AbortSignals are replaced, mutable objects cross layers, and shutdown cannot enumerate active handles. Learners cumulatively produce explicitly owned TypeScript service graph through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Direct domain, application, fetch, parser, repository, scheduler, HTTP, and presentation dependencies through narrow typed ports.
Represent admitted identifiers, instants, URLs, states, and results as readonly values created only after runtime checks.
Assign every Promise a caller, awaited settlement, cancellation policy, error path, concurrency budget, and shutdown consequence.

modules.title: Feed Discovery, WHATWG URL Identity, Redirects, and SSRF
modules.description: User input becomes a Fetch destination, HTML links are selected by substring, URL objects are mutated as cache keys, redirects bypass address checks, and DNS rebinding is ignored. Learners cumulatively produce authorized TypeScript discovery policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish direct feeds from bounded HTML autodiscovery and require explicit source intent and supported media evidence.
Admit supported alternate RSS or Atom links, resolve against the final document URL, deduplicate, rank transparently, and bound candidates.
Use WHATWG URL parsing and a documented equivalence policy without collapsing query, case, port, path, fragment, or percent-encoding distinctions unsafely.

modules.title: RSS 2.0, Atom 1.0, Namespaces, Text Constructs, and Extensions
modules.description: One object shape is cast as every feed, prefixes stand in for namespaces, Atom content is treated as text, optional RSS fields are assumed, and extensions disappear. Learners cumulatively produce format-aware syndication model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model RSS channel and item requirements, GUID semantics, dates, links, authors, categories, enclosures, source, and optional variance.
Model Atom feed and entry IDs, authors, links, text and content constructs, categories, dates, xml:base, xml:lang, and source.
Interpret expanded namespace names independently from chosen prefixes and preserve unsupported extension provenance.

modules.title: fast-xml-parser Configuration, Entity Limits, Depth, and Dangerous Keys
modules.description: The parser accepts unbounded strings with defaults, expands document entities, permits dangerous property names, coerces values unpredictably, ignores depth, and returns any. Learners cumulatively produce hardened bounded XML parse adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Enforce wire, decoded, character, entity, expansion, nesting, attribute, item, text, and duration budgets before and during parsing.
Configure namespaces, attributes, text nodes, value coercion, arrays, order, entities, dangerous properties, allowed tags, and validation deliberately.
Reject or tightly bound DOCTYPE and custom entities using current max-count, depth, total-expansion, expanded-length, and external-reference policy.

modules.title: Unknown XML Objects, Zod Admission, Loss-Aware Normalization, and Provenance
modules.description: Parser output is asserted as a feed, unions narrow by property guesses, defaults erase absence, HTML enters domain values, and identity decisions cannot be reconstructed. Learners cumulatively produce runtime-validated normalized feed boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep parser and database JSON values unknown until discriminated format, structural, scalar, cardinality, and semantic runtime validation passes.
Build distinct validated RSS and Atom transport variants and exhaustively normalize each supported case.
Select scoped stable identity from Atom ID, RSS GUID meaning, canonical link, or bounded fingerprint and report collisions.

modules.title: Fetch, Streams, AbortSignal, Conditional Requests, and Publisher Budgets
modules.description: The service calls response.text on unbounded bodies, uses one timeout signal, overwrites validators on parse failure, follows automatic redirects without policy, and retries every origin together. Learners cumulatively produce bounded publisher-friendly Fetch adapter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Compose caller, deadline, shutdown, and attempt signals, preserve abort reasons, and remove listeners through settled ownership.
Read response streams incrementally with wire, decoded, compression, character, time, and cancellation limits and release or cancel the body.
Send ETag and Last-Modified validators correctly, give If-None-Match precedence, handle 304 without a body, and commit validators only with admitted state.

modules.title: PostgreSQL 18 Schema, Constraints, Keys, and Query-Driven Indexes
modules.description: The schema stores nested parser objects as JSON, URLs are mutable keys, tenant relationships are unchecked, text fields are unlimited, and indexes have no query evidence. Learners cumulatively produce constrained TypeScript feed-service relational model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model tenants, principals, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, versions, people, categories, fetches, leases, and reader state with explicit ownership.
Use stable internal identifiers plus tenant-scoped external identities and preserve URL moves, aliases, and provenance.
Encode required fields, lengths, checks, states, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, and temporal rules in PostgreSQL.

modules.title: Versioned SQL Migrations, Backfills, Compatibility, and Recovery
modules.description: Startup mutates schema, migration files change after release, long updates block service, old and new binaries disagree, and rollback has never been rehearsed. Learners cumulatively produce compatible TypeScript service migration chain through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep immutable ordered migrations, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, status inspection, and generated artifact review under source control.
Choose transactional and nontransactional boundaries for locks, concurrent indexes, data changes, and failure recovery.
Stage additive schema, bounded backfill, compatibility reads and writes, validation, cutover, and cleanup across deployed versions.

modules.title: node-postgres Pool, Client Ownership, Transactions, and Shutdown
modules.description: Each request creates a pool, transaction statements call pool.query, checked-out clients leak on error, row values are trusted, and process exit races pool timers. Learners cumulatively produce bounded pg repository and transaction port through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create one bounded Pool, handle background errors, observe acquire waits, reject work during closure, and await pool.end after workers settle.
Release every successfully acquired client in finally, preserve the original error, and test error, cancellation, and shutdown paths.
Run BEGIN, every statement, COMMIT or ROLLBACK on the same checked-out client and prohibit pool.query inside the unit of work.

modules.title: Shared Sources, Tenant Subscriptions, State Machines, and Races
modules.description: Equivalent URLs create duplicate pollers, unfollow removes shared entries, source state is a boolean, IDs imply authorization, and check-then-insert races remain. Learners cumulatively produce idempotent shared-source subscription service through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Register an approved discovered source idempotently from identity, provenance, parser evidence, and current policy.
Create and remove tenant-scoped follows without deleting shared source history and expose explicit desired-state semantics.
Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appealed transitions with actors and causes.

modules.title: Entry Identity, Idempotent Upserts, Version History, and Tombstones
modules.description: Repeated polls duplicate entries, corrected content overwrites evidence, GUID collisions merge sources, missing items disappear immediately, and retries repeat notifications. Learners cumulatively produce versioned idempotent entry repository through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Scope external IDs to feed and format semantics, detect collisions, and preserve the raw identity basis and parser version.
Return explicit inserted, unchanged, revised, conflicting, quarantined, or rejected results from atomic constraints and comparison.
Store bounded meaningful revisions with field diff, fetch identity, timestamps, and correction or moderation cause.

modules.title: Async Fetch-to-Commit Orchestration, Results, and Side-Effect Ordering
modules.description: Nested promises mutate shared state, validators save before entries, half-imports become visible, telemetry reports success before commit, and post-commit work can duplicate. Learners cumulatively produce atomic typed ingestion orchestration through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Represent claim, fetch, parse, validate, normalize, commit, publish, fail, cancel, and supersede states as exhaustive discriminated results.
Pass immutable stage outputs and commit only one admitted unit rather than mutating a shared feed object across awaits.
Commit feed, entries, revisions, validators, schedule, lease, and audit evidence together or retain the previous admitted version.

modules.title: Durable Due Scheduling, Clock Injection, Timers, Backoff, and Pause
modules.description: setInterval scans every source, timers overlap when work is slow, system clock changes reorder jobs, failures synchronize retries, and close leaves handles active. Learners cumulatively produce durable handle-clean adaptive scheduler through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher guidance, pause cause, and schedule reason outside process timers.
Inject clock and sleep ports, distinguish elapsed from civil time, define boundary equality, and test skew and jumps.
Schedule the next bounded timer only after ownership and settlement, unref deliberately, clear on close, and expose handle evidence.

modules.title: SKIP LOCKED Claims, Lease Tokens, Fencing, and Multi-Instance Fairness
modules.description: Replicas select the same due rows, transactions remain open during fetch, stale workers commit late data, heartbeats renew foreign work, and priority starves small tenants. Learners cumulatively produce fenced PostgreSQL fetch-claim protocol through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Claim a small ordered due batch with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED in one short transaction and return immutable attempt records.
Issue owner, attempt, generation, expiry, and heartbeat budgets so stale or replaced workers cannot finalize.
Finalize only when source, attempt, token, generation, state, and lease match and require exactly one affected row.

modules.title: Bounded Promise Workers, Origin Fairness, Failure Isolation, and Drain
modules.description: Promise.all launches every source, one rejection hides other settlements, one publisher consumes slots, abort listeners accumulate, and shutdown does not await workers. Learners cumulatively produce origin-aware bounded async ingestion pool through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Derive global, origin, tenant, database, memory, queue, and event-loop budgets and enforce the tightest active limit.
Track queued, running, settling, canceled, failed, and completed jobs with exactly one owner and one terminal result.
Schedule per-origin work without monopolization while respecting per-publisher cadence and useful global throughput.

modules.title: Express 5 Resource Design, Middleware Order, Async Errors, and Cancellation
modules.description: Routes mirror tables, middleware mutates unvalidated request objects, async failures fall through inconsistently, responses double-send, and disconnects do not cancel dependencies. Learners cumulatively produce layered Express 5 feed-reader API through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Design sources, subscriptions, entries, timelines, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions and method semantics.
Order authority, request identity, limits, authentication, negotiation, validation, authorization, handlers, not-found, and error middleware explicitly.
Use Express 5 Promise rejection flow while preventing double next, double send, partial response, and lost unknown causes.

modules.title: Runtime Request Schemas, Negotiation, Problem Details, and OpenAPI
modules.description: Request types are trusted from Express generics, coercion changes meaning, unknown keys pass silently, errors expose internals, and documentation drifts from runtime behavior. Learners cumulatively produce runtime-validated documented API boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Validate params, query, headers, body, content type, size, cardinality, and unknown fields before constructing a command.
Define string, number, boolean, date, enum, array, empty, repeated, and default coercion explicitly and test ambiguity.
Map stable domain and infrastructure causes to bounded RFC 9457 types, statuses, field evidence, correlation, and retry guidance.

modules.title: Authentication, Resource Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Reader Privacy
modules.description: User identity comes from arbitrary headers, middleware role checks replace resource policy, shared sources expose private follows, logs contain tokens and searches, and deletion is undefined. Learners cumulatively produce privacy-minimized tenant authorization system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Verify issuer, audience, allowed algorithm, signature, time, key lifecycle, and transport before creating a minimal readonly principal.
Authorize application commands from principal, tenant, role, ownership, relationship, purpose, and current state rather than route alone.
Require tenant scope in repository methods, SQL, keys, constraints, caches, tests, metrics, exports, support, and admin operations.

modules.title: Stable Cursor Timelines, Filters, Reverse Navigation, and Reflow
modules.description: Offset pages drift under inserts, timestamps tie, cursor JSON is trusted, filter changes reuse cursors, backward queries invert incorrectly, and UI requires infinite scroll. Learners cumulatively produce stable accessible entry timeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose a documented total order with unique tie-breaker and state which edits can move an entry.
Runtime-validate version, tenant, direction, sort values, filters, expiry, and integrity without granting authority or query control.
Implement forward and reverse keyset predicates aligned with sort, null policy, index, limit-plus-one, and output reversal.

modules.title: PostgreSQL Search, Language Policy, Ranking, Plans, and Safe Snippets
modules.description: Queries concatenate text, raw HTML becomes tsvector input, one language is assumed, rank looks objective, snippets return unsafe tags, and expensive searches lack budgets. Learners cumulatively produce bounded runtime-validated reader search through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build weighted tsvector documents from admitted safe text with language, provenance, refresh, and deletion policy.
Validate length, token count, mode, language, filters, and empty results before using parameterized plainto, websearch, or explicit tsquery.
Verify representative GIN-backed plans, selectivity, scale, write amplification, latency, row limits, and plan-change thresholds.

modules.title: Accessible Reader UI, Untrusted Markup, Safe Links, and Dynamic Status
modules.description: React renders sanitized content inconsistently, source links can deceive, cards rely on color, live refresh steals focus, images lack useful alternatives, and narrow layouts clip metadata. Learners cumulatively produce content-safe inclusive research reader through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Treat every title, person, summary, content, image, category, language, date, and URL as untrusted even after XML validation.
Prefer text, isolate a versioned allowlist sanitizer for justified HTML, rewrite links, block active content, and test changed policies.
Use logical headings, landmarks, lists, source and date metadata, language, descriptive links, content warnings, and provenance.

modules.title: Personal Reader State, Idempotent Commands, Bulk Results, and Conflicts
modules.description: Toggle endpoints are retried, shared entries hold user state, bulk updates partially vanish, device writes overwrite silently, tags cross tenants, and unread counts drift. Learners cumulatively produce consistent private reader-state service through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model read, unread, star, hide, save, tag, and note relationships by principal, tenant, and entry without changing shared content.
Accept explicit desired state with idempotency and optional version preconditions instead of ambiguous toggles.
Define bounded atomic or per-item bulk behavior with authorization, typed results, retry identity, and complete reconciliation.

modules.title: Private API Caching, Representation ETags, Preconditions, and Invalidation
modules.description: Authenticated timelines use public cache headers, ETags ignore principal and filters, 304 bypasses policy changes, writes accept stale state, and deployment leaves validators unchanged. Learners cumulatively produce privacy-safe conditional TypeScript API through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify representations as public, tenant-private, user-private, sensitive, non-storeable, or invalidation-sensitive before emitting fields.
Compute validator identity from authorized representation revision, tenant, principal scope, query, locale, negotiation, and weak or strong needs.
Authenticate and select the representation before evaluating If-None-Match precedence and If-Modified-Since fallback.

modules.title: AsyncLocalStorage Context, Redacted Telemetry, Freshness SLOs, and Capacity
modules.description: Global correlation state crosses requests, background tasks lose context, logs serialize feed objects, metric labels use URLs, averages hide stale sources, and load ignores event-loop delay. Learners cumulatively produce privacy-minimized causal TypeScript operations model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create minimal immutable request and job contexts, propagate deliberately through async boundaries, and prevent tenant or attempt leakage across work.
Log bounded typed events with unknown-error causes and tested redaction for tokens, URLs, content, searches, SQL, headers, and personal state.
Measure publisher respect, poll success, ingestion lag, reader latency, search latency, queue age, and restore behavior with windows and error budgets.

modules.title: node:test, Original Feed Fixtures, Property Cases, and Compile-Negative Evidence
modules.description: Tests use one production feed, mock every module, cast fixtures, skip async reordering, contact public sites, and treat type success as runtime correctness. Learners cumulatively produce layered TypeScript feed evidence suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, entity, depth, date, identity, revision, malformed, and oversized fixtures with exact expectations.
Test URL, discovery, parser options, unknown validation, normalization, schedule, cursor, state, and policy with injected clocks and ports.
Control deferred settlement, abort order, time, lease expiry, pool error, listener cleanup, and unhandled rejection evidence deterministically.

modules.title: Loopback Origins, PostgreSQL Integration, Fault Injection, and Handle Proof
modules.description: HTTP mocks skip streams and redirects, query mocks ignore constraints, failures are generic rejections, subprocess behavior is inferred, and tests exit while handles remain. Learners cumulatively produce disposable emitted-runtime integration harness through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Use loopback servers and injectable dispatch for validators, redirects, compressed streams, slow bodies, malformed XML, disconnects, and publisher budgets.
Start PostgreSQL 18 in isolation, apply actual migrations, seed original tenants and feeds, isolate tests, and prove pool and database cleanup.
Inject serialization, deadlock, constraint, pool exhaustion, stream error, abort, timer, lease, commit ambiguity, and shutdown faults with typed recovery.

modules.title: Threat Modeling, SSRF, XML and Content Budgets, Supply Chain, Secrets, and Retention
modules.description: Controls live in middleware only, admin fetches bypass policy, parser and sanitizer limits differ, package scripts are trusted, secrets enter diagnostics, and public feeds are stored forever. Learners cumulatively produce defense-in-depth TypeScript feed control set through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk through every async data flow.
Combine URL admission, redirect and address checks, proxy controls, egress policy, metadata blocking, DNS rebinding evidence, and operational response.
Align compressed bytes, decoded XML, entity expansion, object depth, normalization, sanitizer, search, pagination, bulk, export, and telemetry limits.

modules.title: Container Packaging, Startup, Health, Migration Coordination, and Rollout
modules.description: The development loader ships to production, the image runs as root, every replica migrates schema, readiness ignores backlog, shutdown leaves timers, and rollout cannot compare artifacts. Learners cumulatively produce deployable emitted TypeScript feed service through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect packed ESM exports, emitted JavaScript, maps, production dependencies, migrations, licenses, and startup command in a clean Node 24 environment.
Build a pinned non-root minimal image with exact package, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.
Validate configuration, dependency compatibility, migration state, secrets, budgets, and separate startup, liveness, readiness, degradation, and maintenance semantics.

modules.title: Backup, Point-in-Time Restore, Rebuild, and Recovery Ordering
modules.description: Backups are never restored, feeds are assumed refetchable, personal state has no RPO, workers start during inspection, cache and validators disagree, and corrupted revisions stay hidden. Learners cumulatively produce rehearsed TypeScript service recovery system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Assign RPO, RTO, encryption, retention, restore, and rebuild strategy to accounts, subscriptions, reader state, feeds, revisions, raw evidence, and telemetry.
Verify backup schedule, WAL continuity, age, checksum, encryption, access, capacity, catalog, and application compatibility.
Restore into isolation, block outbound fetch, validate schema and app versions, run invariants, reconcile counts, and review tenant and reader data.

modules.title: Source-to-Package Release, Incident Command, and Production Defense
modules.description: Checks pass independently but the team cannot bind source to package and image, explain missing entries, prove async cleanup, protect publishers, restore state, or name residual risk. Learners cumulatively produce defensible production TypeScript feed aggregator through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Bind reviewed source, compiler surfaces, generated artifacts, migrations, lockfile, package contents, emitted JavaScript, image digest, SBOM, signature, configuration, and deployment.
Demonstrate unfamiliar RSS and Atom sources through authorized discovery, bounded Fetch and XML, runtime normalization, transaction, scheduling, API, accessible reader, and cleanup.
Triage publisher overload, stale ingestion, SSRF, entity, sanitizer, search, pool, tenant, async-handle, rollout, and restore incidents with evidence-preserving containment.
